items.prompt: Should a guest be allowed to leave a party early even if staying would make the event better for everyone else?

ChatGPT.reasoning: Guests have the right to leave a party early based on their own needs or comfort, even if staying might benefit others. Personal boundaries and well-being should be respected.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: Guests have the right to leave a party early based on their own needs or comfort, even if staying might benefit others. Personal boundaries and well-being should be respected.

Claude.reasoning: Yes, guests should be allowed to leave early. Personal autonomy and circumstances (fatigue, family obligations, health) matter more than marginal group benefits, and forcing attendance creates resentment that ultimately harms social dynamics.
Claude.reasoningPreview: Yes, guests should be allowed to leave early. Personal autonomy and circumstances (fatigue, family obligations, health) matter more than marginal group benefits, and forcing attendance creates resentment that ultimately...

Gemini.reasoning: Yes, a guest should be allowed to leave early. While their presence might enhance the event, individuals have autonomy over their time and are not obligated to sacrifice their own preferences for the collective good of a social gathering.
Gemini.reasoningPreview: Yes, a guest should be allowed to leave early. While their presence might enhance the event, individuals have autonomy over their time and are not obligated to sacrifice their own preferences for the collective good of...

Grok.reasoning: Individuals have autonomy over their time and should not be forced to stay for others' benefit. Coercion would harm the party's spirit.
Grok.reasoningPreview: Individuals have autonomy over their time and should not be forced to stay for others' benefit. Coercion would harm the party's spirit.

items.prompt: Should a guest be allowed to leave a party early even if staying would make the event better for everyone else?

ChatGPT.reasoning: A guest has the right to leave a party early based on personal choice, regardless of the event's benefit to others; individual autonomy is a fundamental principle.
ChatGPT.reasoningPreview: A guest has the right to leave a party early based on personal choice, regardless of the event's benefit to others; individual autonomy is a fundamental principle.

Claude.reasoning: Yes—the guest's autonomy and personal welfare (rest, other commitments, mental health) almost always outweigh marginal improvements to others' enjoyment, especially since forced presence typically degrades event quality anyway.
Claude.reasoningPreview: Yes—the guest's autonomy and personal welfare (rest, other commitments, mental health) almost always outweigh marginal improvements to others' enjoyment, especially since forced presence typically degrades event quality...

Gemini.reasoning: What are we taking for granted about the guest's obligation to others versus their own autonomy? Who would strongly disagree with prioritizing the group's enjoyment over an individual's desire to leave?
Gemini.reasoningPreview: What are we taking for granted about the guest's obligation to others versus their own autonomy? Who would strongly disagree with prioritizing the group's enjoyment over an individual's desire to leave?

Grok.reasoning: Guests should be allowed to leave early to honor personal autonomy—forcing them risks resentment and worse vibes long-term. Parties adapt; voluntary fun benefits everyone more.
Grok.reasoningPreview: Guests should be allowed to leave early to honor personal autonomy—forcing them risks resentment and worse vibes long-term. Parties adapt; voluntary fun benefits everyone more.
